evidence: skills/classify/references/classify_reference.py:35-42, skills/regress/references/regress_reference.py:21-28, skills/cluster/references/cluster_reference.py:22-28, skills/anomaly/references/anomaly_reference.py:20-26 all use `_PLUGIN_ROOT = _HERE.parents[3]` and `sys.path.insert(0, _HERE.parent)` then sibling-imports `preprocessing` / `model_zoo` / `_shared`. The SKILLs instruct copy-to-.mltoolkit/session.py but the copied file cannot resolve these imports. tests/test_classify.py:40-56 runs in place so the break is not covered.
recommendation: Inline preprocessing.py and model_zoo.py into each reference before copy (or stage-copy the whole references/ directory) and add a CI test that copies session.py to a temp dir and executes it.

evidence: README.md:21 and skills/tune/SKILL.md:24-26 advertise optuna as an alternative backend; repo-wide grep finds only README text, SKILL prose, references/_shared/deps.py:41-42 has_optuna(), and scripts/check-env.sh:9. No optuna code path exists.
recommendation: Implement an optuna tuner behind `--search-library optuna` gated on deps.has_optuna(), or remove the advertising copy from README.md and skills/tune/SKILL.md.

evidence: skills/classify/references/classify_reference.py:143-183 evaluate() computes a global report only; no --group-col / --sensitive-col; protected attributes flow through preprocessing.py:23-63 silently. No mltoolkit equivalent of PyCaret's check_fairness (capability §1.15, §6.1).
recommendation: Add references/_shared/fairness.py with group_metrics(y_true,y_pred,y_proba,groups) returning per-group AUC, PR-AUC, PPV/NPV, sensitivity, specificity, FPR/FNR plus disparities; wire --group-col (alias --sensitive-col) into classify_reference and regress_reference; emit results/fairness_report.csv and a bar-plot figure.
disagreement_note: DS scored P2 for fairness; PH scored P0. Chose P0 — target audience is public-health / clinical-prediction users per plugin goals, and protected-attribute leakage is the largest audience-facing gap.

evidence: skills/classify/references/classify_reference.py:143-183 produces CM / ROC / PR / permutation importance only. No CalibratedClassifierCV, reliability diagram, Brier score, ECE, or calibration intercept/slope. TRIPOD+AI 15/16 unreportable. Capability §1.6 documents calibrate_model.
recommendation: Add references/_shared/calibration.py producing reliability diagram, Brier, ECE, intercept/slope; expose --calibrate {isotonic,sigmoid,none} wrapping the tuned estimator in CalibratedClassifierCV(cv='prefit'); emit results/calibration.json and artifacts/calibration_curve.png.
disagreement_note: DS P1, PH P0. Chose P0 — calibration is both a PyCaret parity gap and a non-negotiable TRIPOD+AI reporting item for the plugin's clinical-prediction audience.

evidence: classify_reference.py:147-148 and regress_reference.py:113-119 write holdout metrics as point estimates; cross_val_score mean/std only (classify_reference.py:100-107) — per-fold scores discarded. No bootstrap CIs or DeLong intervals. TRIPOD+AI 13b requires 95% CIs.
recommendation: Add bootstrap_ci(metric_fn, y_true, y_pred, n_boot=1000) to references/_shared/; emit results/holdout_metrics_ci.json with 95% CIs and leaderboard_folds.csv with one row per (model, fold).
disagreement_note: DS P2, PH P0. Chose P0 — paper-ready reporting requires CIs; effort is moderate and unblocks multiple downstream claims.

evidence: classify_reference.py:203-207 and regress_reference.py:171 use plain train_test_split(random_state=42). No GroupKFold, StratifiedGroupKFold, or TimeSeriesSplit option. Clinical / longitudinal / multi-site data leak across splits.
recommendation: Add --group-col (→ GroupKFold / StratifiedGroupKFold) and --time-col (→ TimeSeriesSplit) flags wired into both cross_val_score and train_test_split; document in SKILL.md.
disagreement_note: DS P1, PH P0. Chose P0 — random-split leakage directly falsifies holdout metrics for the plugin's target audience.

evidence: skills/setup/SKILL.md:20-37 has no step prompting for IRB/consent, protected-attribute list, dataset provenance; no datasheet.md stub is emitted.
recommendation: Extend setup SKILL.md to require (a) IRB/consent status, (b) protected-attribute column list, (c) data provenance statement; emit .mltoolkit/datasheet.md stub.

evidence: skills/classify/references/preprocessing.py:50-55 fits TargetEncoder against y on any high-cardinality column including zip_code/county_fips-style neighbourhood proxies — silent outcome leak into proxy-discrimination vectors.
recommendation: Refuse to TargetEncode columns that appear in --sensitive-features or match a protected-attribute dictionary (race, ethnicity, gender, zip, county, ...); require an explicit --allow-target-encoding-on <col> override.

evidence: classify_reference.py:118 (n_iter=20) and :135 (scoring='accuracy' regardless of compare's ranking metric); regress_reference.py:88,101. No --n-iter, --scoring, --cv-tune, --time-budget flags. SKILL.md:58 says 'may require editing the script'.
recommendation: Promote n_iter, scoring, cv-tune, time-budget to argparse flags; default --tune-metric to compare's leaderboard ranking metric so winners agree.

evidence: classify_reference.py:167-183 emits permutation_importance only. No SHAP, PartialDependenceDisplay, calibration_curve, or learning_curve. Capability §1.9 / §6.2 flag SHAP+PDP as PyCaret core.
recommendation: Add SHAP summary + beeswarm gated on `shap` installed (fall back to permutation), PartialDependenceDisplay top-k, calibration_curve, and learning_curve to the evaluate stage; emit artifacts/shap_summary.png + results/shap_values.csv.

evidence: skills/classify/SKILL.md:50,59 tells Claude to 'mention SMOTE' and 'edit session.py' but no code wraps the pipeline in imblearn.pipeline.Pipeline; sklearn Pipeline/ColumnTransformer mro makes the hand-edit non-trivial.
recommendation: Implement --resample {smote,adasyn,none} wrapping (pre, model) in imblearn.pipeline.Pipeline when deps.has_imblearn(); document in SKILL.md.

evidence: skills/classify/SKILL.md:50 mentions a 4:1 heuristic but classify_reference.py:56-81 run_eda computes no numeric minority prevalence; no events-per-variable (EPV) check anywhere (PROBAST 1.4 / Riley et al. guidance commonly require EPV≥10).
recommendation: Extend run_eda to compute minority prevalence, events-per-variable (EPV = min_class_count / n_features_after_preprocessing); emit results/epv_warning.json and a loud stdout warning when EPV<10 or prevalence<5%; cite Riley et al.

evidence: skills/classify/references/model_zoo.py:22-85 ships 11 sklearn models. Capability §2.1 enumerates 19 canonical IDs. Missing qda, lda, gpc, rbfsvm, dummy, par, MultinomialNB, BernoulliNB. Dummy baseline is load-bearing for PH prevalence-floor framing.
recommendation: Add QuadraticDiscriminantAnalysis, LinearDiscriminantAnalysis, DummyClassifier(strategy='stratified'), PassiveAggressiveClassifier, MultinomialNB, BernoulliNB; skip GPC by default (quadratic memory).

evidence: skills/regress/references/model_zoo.py:22-53 ships 12 models; capability §3.1 lists 23. Missing lar, llar, omp, br, ard, par, ransac, tr (Theil-Sen), huber, kr, dummy. Robust regressors and a DummyRegressor floor matter for PH cost/utilization data.
recommendation: Add LassoLars, LassoLarsIC, OrthogonalMatchingPursuit, BayesianRidge, ARDRegression, PassiveAggressiveRegressor, RANSACRegressor, TheilSenRegressor, HuberRegressor, KernelRidge, DummyRegressor(strategy='mean'); flag robust estimators in leaderboard.md.

evidence: skills/cluster/references/model_zoo.py:7-16 ships 4 algorithms; capability §4.1 lists 9. Missing ap, meanshift, sc, optics, birch, kmodes.
recommendation: Add AffinityPropagation, MeanShift, SpectralClustering, OPTICS, Birch; gate kmodes via deps.has_kmodes().

evidence: skills/anomaly/references/model_zoo.py:8-14 ships 4 detectors; capability §5.1 lists ~12. Missing pca (reconstruction), MinCovDet (mcd), HBOS, KNN-anomaly, COF, ABOD, SOD, SOS — HBOS/ABOD used in PH surveillance and billing-anomaly work.
recommendation: Add pca (sklearn.decomposition.PCA reconstruction-error wrapper) and mcd (sklearn.covariance.MinCovDet) sklearn-natively; gate abod/hbos/cof/sod/sos via deps.has_pyod().

evidence: classify_reference.py:143-183 emits CM / ROC / PR / permutation only. Capability §2.2 lists 15 canonical plots — missing threshold, class_report, boundary, rfe, learning, manifold, calibration, vc, gain, lift, ks.
recommendation: At minimum add learning_curve, calibration_curve, class_report heatmap, and cumulative gain/lift to evaluate — these are paper-standard.

evidence: No run_manifest.json / environment.json anywhere; best_params.json stores str(v) (classify_reference.py:138-139, regress_reference.py:104-105), not JSON-typed. tier_b_transform.py:53-55 emits unpinned deps. TRIPOD+AI 7a/19b unfulfilled.
recommendation: Write .mltoolkit/results/run_manifest.json at each stage with python/sklearn/pandas/numpy/xgboost versions, OS, CPU, git hash, random_state, split strategy, CV folds, JSON-typed hyperparameters; pin requirements.txt via importlib.metadata.version at package time.

evidence: classify_reference.py:220-227 refits on X_train only (80%) before joblib.dump. No finalize_model equivalent. Capability §1.11.
recommendation: Add an optional --finalize stage that retrains best_model on X_train ∪ X_test and writes model_final.joblib alongside model.joblib; update Tier-B/C predict docs to prefer the finalized artifact.
disagreement_note: DS P1, PH P2. Chose P1 — needed for deployment-grade Tier C and TRIPOD+AI expectation; effort is small.

evidence: anomaly_reference.py:101-102 silently skips joblib.dump for LocalOutlierFactor; tier_b_readme_template.md:34-38 and tier_c_scaffold/src/predict.py still tell users to joblib.load('output/model.joblib'). Results in FileNotFoundError at package time.
recommendation: Refit LOF with novelty=True for serialization when --novelty is passed, or emit load_hint.json directing predict.py to refit-on-load when the pickle is absent; document caveat in SKILL.md and README.

evidence: cluster_reference.py:119-127 creates a fresh estimator, refits, then `try: joblib.dump(...) except Exception: pass`. DBSCAN / AgglomerativeClustering silently fail; labels saved to assigned.csv come from an earlier fit so the reloaded model may produce different labels.
recommendation: Save the actually-fitted estimator (not a fresh one); catch only (TypeError, PicklingError) and log to stderr + write a marker file; for DBSCAN document non-serializable-predict behavior and save labels only; ensure random_state is honored on any re-fit path.
disagreement_note: DS P2, PH P1. Chose P1 — PH's reproducibility concern (labels-may-differ-on-reload) outweighs DS's pure-exception-swallowing read; for cohort-assignment studies this is publishable-result-critical.

evidence: skills/package/references/tier_c_scaffold/src/train.py:1-43 has a hardcoded RandomForestClassifier with a comment to 'swap in your chosen model'; inference parity with the in-session pipeline is not automated.
recommendation: Tier-C transform should serialize the chosen estimator from session.py into src/train.py (or emit src/best_estimator.py importable by train.py) so parity is code-enforced, not prose-enforced.

evidence: tests/test_classify.py:41 runs the reference script in place; tests/test_package.py:10-24 uses a trivial synthetic session that never imports preprocessing/model_zoo. The P0 sibling-import bug (LEAD-001) is therefore invisible to CI.
recommendation: Add an end-to-end test that copies classify_reference.py → tmp/.mltoolkit/session.py, runs it against synthetic data, and asserts leaderboard.csv; add a Tier-A package-and-execute test.

evidence: classify_reference.py:145 uses default-0.5 predict(); no optimize_threshold / Youden-J / cost-weighted sweep. Capability §2.5 documents PyCaret's optimize_threshold. For screening/triage clinical papers this is load-bearing.
recommendation: Add --optimize-threshold {youden,f1,mcc,cost,fixed-recall} writing results/threshold.json and re-computing CM + ROC/PR annotations at the chosen threshold.
disagreement_note: DS P2, PH P1. Chose P1 — threshold tuning is standard in the plugin's clinical-prediction audience.

evidence: skills/classify/references/preprocessing.py:37-49 and skills/regress/references/preprocessing.py:33-48 hard-code SimpleImputer(strategy='median'|'most_frequent') unconditionally. No IterativeImputer/KNNImputer, no missingness audit, no MAR/MCAR/MNAR disclosure, no missing-indicator columns.
recommendation: Add --imputation {simple,iterative,knn,drop,multiple} option; emit results/missingness.csv and artifacts/missingness_pattern.png; document MAR/MCAR/MNAR awareness in SKILL.md.
disagreement_note: DS P2, PH P1. Chose P1 — missing-data handling is a mandatory paper-reporting field.

evidence: classify_reference.py:143-183 — classification_report.csv gives per-class metrics only; no per-(user-supplied-subgroup) metric stratification.
recommendation: When --group-col is passed, emit results/subgroup_metrics.csv with each group's AUC/PR/PPV/NPV + sample size + prevalence; warn when any subgroup n < N-MIN.
disagreement_note: DS P2, PH P1. Chose P1 — subgroup metrics are required for equity-aware ML reporting.

evidence: skills/setup/references/setup_reference.py:35-41 only writes schema.csv; grep confirms table1/tableone/SMD absent. No cohort-characteristics Table 1 anywhere.
recommendation: Add references/_shared/table1.py generating per-covariate mean±SD / N(%) overall and by outcome with SMD (and p-values when applicable); call from classify_reference.run_eda and regress_reference.run_eda; emit results/table1.csv.
disagreement_note: DS P2, PH P1. Chose P1 — Table 1 is required in clinical ML papers.

evidence: skills/package/references/tier_b_readme_template.md:1-39 and skills/package/references/tier_c_transform.py:38-54 emit no reports/methods.md, no tripod_ai_checklist.md, no supplement/ directory.
recommendation: Tier B/C: emit reports/methods.md (split strategy, preprocessing, zoo tried, CV folds, tuning budget, seeds, versions) and reports/tripod_ai_checklist.md with auto-populated rows.

evidence: classify_reference.py:143-183 has no decision-curve / net-benefit plot; grep confirms 'decision' absent from code.
recommendation: Add references/_shared/decision_curve.py (net-benefit vs threshold) via a --decision-curve flag; emit artifacts/decision_curve.png.

evidence: skills/anomaly/SKILL.md:17-41 has no warning about false-positive harms when flagging fraud / welfare-abuse / clinical outliers in protected subgroups; no --group-col to check anomaly-rate disparity.
recommendation: Add ethics warning block to anomaly SKILL.md; when --group-col is passed, emit results/anomaly_rate_by_group.csv with disparity ratios.

evidence: classify_reference.py:109 sorts leaderboard by roc_auc only; no PPV@sensitivity, NPV@specificity, NNT, or net-benefit columns.
recommendation: Extend compare_models scorers dict with PPV@recall-0.8, specificity@recall-0.8, and Brier; add a --leaderboard-sort flag; document clinical-utility interpretation in leaderboard.md.

evidence: cluster_reference.py:31-37 and anomaly_reference.py:29-35 silently drop non-numeric columns via select_dtypes(include='number'); skills/cluster/SKILL.md:38-40 promises a user prompt but no code emits one.
recommendation: Print a stderr warning listing dropped columns; add --categorical {drop,one-hot} option wiring non-numeric columns through a ColumnTransformer consistent with classify/regress preprocessing.
disagreement_note: DS P2, PH P1. Chose P1 — silently dropping indicator-encoded protected attributes is an audit-failure path.

evidence: No MLflow / W&B / comet_ml hook anywhere; capability §6.3 documents PyCaret's log_experiment='mlflow'. Current tracking surface is leaderboard.csv + best_params.json + holdout_metrics.json.
recommendation: Add optional --log-mlflow (alias --track {none,mlflow}) flag to setup / classify / regress reference that starts an mlflow run and logs params, metrics, artifacts when mlflow is installed.

evidence: skills/package/references/tier_b_transform.py:37-55 and tier_c_scaffold/requirements.txt:1-9 emit unpinned requirements; two users one month apart receive different versions.
recommendation: At package time, capture importlib.metadata.version(pkg) for every detected import and pin pkg==version; expose --pin {exact,compatible,none}.

evidence: classify_reference.py:89-95 records accuracy, f1[_macro], roc_auc(binary) only. Capability §2.4 canonical: add MCC, Cohen's kappa, balanced_accuracy, logloss, average_precision, brier_score.
recommendation: Add MCC, kappa, balanced_accuracy, logloss, average_precision as additional leaderboard columns.

evidence: regress_reference.py:71-77 produces R², RMSE, MAE. Capability §3.4 canonical: add MAPE, RMSLE, explained_variance, median_absolute_error.
recommendation: Add MAPE, RMSLE (guard non-negative targets), and explained_variance to the leaderboard.

evidence: No VotingClassifier / StackingClassifier / BaggingClassifier wrappers; Capability §1.5 covers ensemble_model / blend_models / stack_models.
recommendation: Add a --ensemble {voting,stacking} stage that consumes the top-k from the leaderboard.

evidence: references/_shared/reporting.py:18-60 defines summary_report producing an HTML dashboard, but no SKILL.md or reference script calls it.
recommendation: Wire summary_report as the final step of every task's evaluate stage; link figures + tables + manifest into one self-contained .mltoolkit/results/report.html.

evidence: regress_reference.py:171 uses plain train_test_split for regression without binned stratification; skewed targets split unevenly.
recommendation: Detect skew (|skew|>1) in EDA and auto-switch to stratify=pd.qcut(y, 10, duplicates='drop') with a warning.

evidence: skills/package/references/tier_b_readme_template.md:1-39 has no intended-use, out-of-scope, re-identification-risk, harm, or dataset-provenance sections.
recommendation: Extend tier_b_readme_template.md with Intended use / Out-of-scope / Training-data provenance / Known biases / Re-identification risk / Maintenance plan sections (minimal model card).

evidence: No STARD 2015 or CONSORT-AI checklist template in skills/package/references/; tier_b_readme_template.md and tier_c_transform.py are silent on reporting-guideline family.
recommendation: Tier B/C: when task is classification and user passes --diagnostic, emit reports/stard_checklist.md; when --interventional, emit reports/consort_ai_checklist.md.
